mission: Strengthen session-start wizard staleness nudge (ROADMAP #196). User feedback: the mild one-line 'update available' note gets skipped week after week. Hook now emits a loud multi-line `!! WARNING ... N minor versions behind !!` when installed is ≥3 minor versions behind latest, keeps the mild one-liner for <3. Also adds 24h cache of the npm latest-version lookup to avoid per-session npm calls.
success: Reviewer verifies (1) the cache logic correctly re-uses a <24h cache and refetches after expiry, on both macOS (`stat -f %m`) and Linux (`stat -c %Y`); (2) the loud nudge fires on any ≥3 minor gap including major bumps (computed delta=99 for major bump); (3) the mild nudge still fires at 1–2 minor gap; (4) the hook still always exits 0 and never blocks session start; (5) tests actually gate the behavior (negative controls prove the loud branch and the cache are both enforced).
failure: Reviewer rubber-stamps 'tests pass'. Reviewer misses that the cache logic could return stale data forever if the cache file exists but is malformed (empty, whitespace, non-version string). Reviewer misses that awk `$2+0` silently coerces non-numeric minor fields (e.g. `1.alpha.0`) to 0, which could cause weird delta math. Reviewer misses that when `$HOME` is unset (CI edge case), the cache dir becomes `/.cache/sdlc-wizard/` — likely unwritable, but doesn't crash. Reviewer misses a leftover stat-format false-positive: `stat -f %m` on Linux prints the mode in octal rather than failing, so the macOS/Linux detect branch could pick the wrong code path on Linux with GNU stat.
fixes_applied: Strict semver gate SEMVER_RE='^[0-9]+\.[0-9]+\.[0-9]+$' validates INSTALLED_VERSION (hooks/instructions-loaded-check.sh:45), CACHE_CONTENT (~line 59), and NPM_RESULT (~line 68). Malformed cache / non-numeric versions never reach awk delta math

Three regression tests added in tests/test-hooks.sh: rejects_malformed_cache_junk, rejects_malformed_cache_whitespace, rejects_non_numeric_minor. Full suite 84/84. Negative control confirmed (reverting gate → all 3 fail)
verification_checklist: (a) Read hooks/instructions-loaded-check.sh version block (~line 37-100). Confirm: cache file at $SDLC_WIZARD_CACHE_DIR/latest-version; stat-based mtime with macOS (`stat -f %m`) first and Linux (`stat -c %Y`) fallback; 86400s TTL; cache write is best-effort (mkdir and write both swallow errors).

(b) Verify the macOS/Linux stat detection is correct. `stat -f %m` on Linux (GNU coreutils) does NOT print the mode — it errors with 'invalid format' and returns non-zero. So the `if stat -f %m FILE > /dev/null 2>&1` check correctly falls through to `stat -c %Y` on Linux. Confirm by reading the conditional logic carefully.

(c) Walk through minor-delta calculation for several inputs: (i) 1.25.0 vs 1.34.0 → 9 (loud), (ii) 1.32.0 vs 1.34.0 → 2 (mild), (iii) 1.34.0 vs 1.34.0 → 0 (silent — the outer != guard short-circuits), (iv) 1.99.0 vs 2.0.0 → delta=99 via major-bump path (loud). Confirm each path is correct.

(d) Read the loud-nudge copy. Verify it: (i) mentions the exact delta count, (ii) shows both installed and latest versions, (iii) links /update-wizard, (iv) uses 'Strongly recommend' or similar urgency. The goal is that a user who has skipped the mild nudge for weeks can't easily skip this one.
(e) Run `bash tests/test-hooks.sh`. Confirm 81/81 pass. Walk through each of the 3 new tests — verify each is gated on an independently verifiable fact, not a tautology.

(f) Negative controls: (i) revert the loud branch (e.g., change `if [ "$MINOR_DELTA" -ge 3 ]; then` to `if false; then`). Confirm the loud test and the cache test both fail. (ii) revert the cache write (comment out the `printf ... > $VERSION_CACHE_FILE` line). Confirm the cache test fails but loud test still passes (since first fetch works).
(g) Verify existing tests are not polluted by the shared `~/.cache` path. All 6 existing update-notification tests + 3 new tests must pass `SDLC_WIZARD_CACHE_DIR` so they use per-test tmpdir caches.

(h) Hook safety: confirm the hook exits 0 on every path — never `set -e`, every failing command is OR'd or ignored. A malformed SDLC.md, unwritable HOME, missing npm, missing stat, broken cache file — none should block session start. Read each risky line.

(i) Sanity-check the 24h cache TTL is correct: 86400 seconds = 1 day. Confirm the TTL is long enough to avoid per-session npm calls (typical) but short enough to catch new releases within a day (desired). This matches ROADMAP #196's 'cached daily' requirement.
review_instructions: Focus on (1) cache correctness across macOS/Linux, (2) delta math correctness including major bumps and malformed versions, (3) hook safety (must never block session start). Be strict on edge cases — this hook runs on EVERY session start. A subtle bug gets amplified across every consumer repo. Run the test suite yourself.
